title: Praxen canonical findings JSON — v2.0
description: Published contract for the Praxen canonical findings JSON (one object per analysis). Praxen's renderer ships with a hand-written runtime validator (`schema.py`) that enforces this shape *plus* cross-field invariants (counts match the arrays, anchors resolve, the six RAISE keys are present, weighted_overall = Σ(score × weight)). This file is the machine-readable contract for downstream tooling. Praxen 0.7.0+.

schema_version.description: Schema version. The renderer accepts 2.x and rejects everything else.

praxen_version.description: Praxen version that produced this file (mirrors .claude-plugin/plugin.json).

workspace.description: Absolute path to the analyzed workspace.

agent_remit_summary.description: 2–4 sentences restating the agent's intended scope; may contain inline <code>/<strong>/<em>.

agent_structure_summary.description: 2–4 sentences describing what was observed in the workspace; may contain inline <code>/<strong>/<em>.

total.description: Must equal len(rules); the runtime validator enforces this.

rule_id.description: R-NN style id; unique within rules[].

rule_text.description: Exact quoted text from the Worker Remit.

finding_id.description: PRAX-... id of the linked finding, or null. The runtime validator asserts every non-null id exists in findings[].

findings.description: May be empty for a genuinely clean agent. Renderer sorts by severity (Critical first) preserving input order within a tier.

summary.description: One-sentence card header; specific, not generic.

description.description: Optional longer-form body; carried in the JSON, surfaced in the report when the deferred L&F work lands (see design/DEFERRED.md). May contain inline <code>/<strong>/<em>.

label.description: Full display name, e.g. 'LLM01 — Prompt Injection' (not just 'LLM01').

policy_rule_ids.description: R-NN id(s) the finding violates, e.g. 'R-03' or 'R-03, R-04'; or null if the finding does not trace to a specific Worker Remit rule (RAISE-category / detection-pattern findings often don't). Null exactly when policy_rule_text is null.

policy_rule_text.description: Exact quoted remit text the finding violates; multiple rules joined with ' / '. Null exactly when policy_rule_ids is null.

evidence.description: Structured evidence items. The renderer formats each as 'file:line — snippet'.

file.description: Path relative to the workspace, or a workspace-relative identifier.

line.description: Line number (1-indexed) or null for file-level evidence. Single integer only — for evidence that spans a range (e.g. a function body), put the start line here and carry the range syntax in `snippet` (e.g. 'request_approval node, lines 197-209 — …').

snippet.description: Short observation or quoted context. Never reprint secrets — see SKILL.md.

recommended_actions.description: One or more concrete actions. Renderer renders a single-item array as text, multi-item as a list.

owasp_llm.description: Canonical primary code LLM01–LLM10, or null. Single value: when a finding spans two LLM categories, the dominant attack pattern goes here and any secondary code goes in tags[] only — never comma-separated, never a list. The full 'CODE — Name' label goes in tags[].

owasp_agentic.description: Canonical primary code ASI01–ASI10, or null. Single value: when a finding spans two ASI categories, the dominant attack pattern goes here and any secondary code goes in tags[] only — never comma-separated, never a list. The full 'CODE — Name' label goes in tags[].

related_findings.description: PRAX-... ids of related findings; may be empty.

escalation.description: alert for Critical/High; log_only for Medium/Low/Informational.

positives.description: Verified positive controls. May be empty.

evidence_path.description: file:line, config key, or similar pointer.

no_logs_note.description: Required non-empty when present=false; may be empty otherwise.

rows.description: Empty exactly when present=false (the runtime validator enforces this).

weighted_overall.description: Σ(score × weight). The runtime validator asserts this matches the per-category sum within rounding.

weighted_rationale.description: 2–4 sentence prose explaining the weighted overall in posture terms.

categories.description: Exactly the six RAISE categories, in any order. Runtime validator enforces canonical render order, exact key/name pairs, and exact standard weights (Zero Trust 0.25; the other five 0.15).

name.description: Display name, e.g. 'Limit Your Domain'. Must match the canonical name for the key.

weight.description: 0.25 for Zero Trust; 0.15 for the other five. The validator enforces the per-key mapping.

rationale.description: 1–2 sentence prose grounded in specific evidence.

severity_counts.description: Must match Counter(f.severity for f in findings) after mapping Informational→info; the runtime validator enforces this.
